marketState: neutral — price above fair value, storage surplus weighs, but extreme forward heat and crowded short positioning set up squeeze risk
summary: Front-month NG=F at $3.256 sits ~$0.30 above the model fair value of $2.958, within the $2.46–$3.46 band but biased toward the upper half across all four forward time horizons. The dominant near-term tension is a split weather picture: 1–7 day demand is 6.5% below the 5-year average (bearish now), but the 8–16 day period is 26.8% above the 5-year average and above the entire historical maximum for that window (rangePos 1.23, aboveMax=true) — a rare extreme heat signal. Storage is a structural bear (+153 Bcf surplus vs 5y, plus a recent +25 Bcf bearish surprise), which limits medium-term upside conviction. However, managed money sits at just the 17th percentile of 5-year net-short positioning (-84,909 contracts), meaning shorts are crowded and a sustained heat confirmation could force rapid short-covering toward the $3.38–$3.46 band top.

longTerm.rationale: Storage running +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a recent +25 Bcf bearish surprise provides a structural lid on the front month. Seasonal probabilities invert bearish by week 4 (only 31% chance higher, typical 4-week move -2.3%) and reliability is flagged as very low (44 pp 10-90th-percentile spread). Price at $3.256 is already above the fair-value midpoint of $2.958 on every forward horizon, and the daily/weekly EMA structure (50 below 200) remains bearish — adding long exposure here offers poor risk-reward given the band top near $3.46–$3.51 is less than 20 cents above current price.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for (a) two consecutive EIA storage reports showing material draws that narrow the 5y surplus below 75 Bcf, OR (b) price retreats to $2.95–$3.05 (near fair-value midpoint) offering a long with minimum 1:2.5 R:R to the band top, OR (c) price breaks and holds above $3.46 (current band top) on rising volume, signalling a structural repricing of the fair-value range higher.

shortTerm.entry: $3.20–$3.26 zone (at market or scale in on any dip toward $3.20 during near-term demand softness)
shortTerm.riskReward: 1:1.7 (from zone midpoint $3.23; risk ~$0.13, reward $0.22 to TP2)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; each $0.01/MMBtu move = 1 tick on a standard 10,000 MMBtu contract
shortTerm.rationale: The 8–16 day weather demand reading of +26.8% vs the 5-year average — and critically above the 5-year MAXIMUM (rangePos 1.23, aboveMax=true) — is the strongest single signal in the dataset and should force price discovery toward the upper fair-value band over the next week. Managed money at the 17th 5-year percentile net-short (-84,909 contracts) means a weather-driven rally could become self-reinforcing through forced covering. Short-term technicals (5m through 4h) are aligned bullish with 50>200 EMA crosses, and the 1-hour MACD is positive; near-term demand softness (1–7D at -6.5% vs 5y) provides the dip opportunity.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price closes below $3.10 on meaningful volume, OR the 8–16 day weather model revises back to within the historical 5-year range, OR next EIA print exceeds +70 Bcf widening the storage surplus.

keyDrivers: 8–16D weather demand above 5-year MAXIMUM (rangePos 1.23) — extreme incoming heat is the primary near-term bull catalyst
Managed money net-short at 17th percentile (5y) with -84,909 net contracts — crowded positioning amplifies squeeze risk on weather confirmation
Storage surplus +153 Bcf vs 5y average plus +25 Bcf bearish injection surprise — structural ceiling on front-month rallies
Price $0.30 above fair value mid ($3.256 vs $2.958); band top resistance at $3.46 limits upside on all forward horizons
Near-term (1–7D) demand 6.5% below 5y average — temporary softness before the extreme heat window arrives at 8–16 days
risks: Weather models revise cooler: the extreme 8–16D heat signal is the entire short-term bull thesis; any downward revision collapses the squeeze setup and exposes price to the storage overhang toward $2.95
Storage builds remain outsized: a second consecutive 70+ Bcf weekly injection would widen the 5y surplus and likely extend managed-money short positioning rather than triggering covering
Seasonal deterioration post-week-3: the 4-week window turns sharply bearish (31% probability higher, -2.3% typical move), limiting the duration of any weather-driven rally
